E=mc^2: Energy equals mass times the square of the speed of light.
Gravity_FreeFall: Objects in orbit are in continuous free fall around Earth.
FallingObjects: In vacuum, all objects fall at the same rate.
Buoyancy: An object floats if its average density is less than the fluid.
HotAir: Heated air is less dense than cooler air.
Conduction: Metals are good conductors of heat.
Doppler: The frequency of a wave changes if source and observer move.
Electricity: Current flows when there is potential difference.
Birds: Birds on a single wire are at the same potential.
Magnetism: Earth behaves like a giant magnet.
Relativity_Time: Time passes differently in strong gravity or high speed.
Speed_Light: Nothing travels faster than light in vacuum.
Counting_Apples: Apples can be counted as discrete objects.
Negative_Numbers: Debts can be represented as negative values.
Equation_Pens: Total cost equals price times quantity.
Circle_Angles: A circle can be divided into equal central angles.
Polyhedra: A soccer ball is made of pentagons and hexagons.
Shadows: Sunlight creates shadows forming right triangles.
Momentum: A moving object resists stopping due to momentum.
Inertia: Objects resist changes in their motion.
Melting_Ice: Carbonated liquids speed melting of ice.
Rate_Change: Calculus studies how quantities change with time.
Instantaneous_Speed: Speedometer shows instantaneous rate of change.
Acceleration: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity.
